author: @karlharvymarx2650
text: It really goes against my grain to ask a serious question about this, but I'm confused about unrolling it on the pvc pipe and then rolling it back on to itself. Is the rolled back portion on your shaft inside the pipe, with the doubled up portion long enough to accommodate your stroke length, and the remainder rolled outside of the pipe to provide a seal?  I think that is what the illustration showed, but I just can't see what is going on in the video, or maybe I'm a little distracted for some reason. It is a little cliche, but maybe if you used a cucumber or zucchini for your shaft the green color would provide needed color contrast.

author: @bikerfirefarter7280
text:
If you are familiar with Liverpool slang you will understand the use of 'flange'.  ;-)
On a serious note, I have made these type of seals using bicycle inner-tubes for water pumps/seals, if the gap is fairly close tolerance they can cope with surprisingly high pressure. They also work in DIY automatic greenhouse window-operator mechanisms. They replace sliding friction with rolling friction, which doesn't increase resistance with increasing pressure. And you don't need potentially contaminating 'lube'; especially handy for potable water pumping, nobody wants a mouthful of gunk.

author: @ogreunderbridge5204
text: Also have a look into air springs (cushions) and air brake chambers (diaphragms). Both may do the same jobs, but offer more space for membrane reinforcement and less internal resistance and wear. To be found in the commercial vehicles section, or industrial automation pneumatic actuators shopping shelves.

author: @wayne975
text:
Another good use for condoms is the rubber band at the top. Remove excess latex and you left with a very good band that I have used in  cassette tape recorders and cd player draw drives.  
Female condoms are huge and will allow bigger pistons.

author: @SeanBZA
text: You do get polyurethane condoms, which are a lot stronger than the regular latex ones, but also have a lot less stretch, so those actually do come in different sizes, because they are not a one size fits all item.  If you want larger you then can get silicone female condoms, which are a lot larger, designed to be folded up into the cylinder wall, instead of on the piston rod.

replies.author: @SeanBZA
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering Buy them, they are free at many places, doctors offices, clinics, banks, government offices.

Health department was giving them away with an anti AIDS leaflet, so, to hold them to the pamphlet, they stapled the condom to the sheet, right through the middle of the foil package.
